root_cause: Control-plane instability cascade is preventing controllers and scheduler from making progress
evidence: Control-plane components degraded together: kube-apiserver, kube-controller-manager, kube-scheduler
Control-plane failure events span 7.0 minutes across 4 corroborating events
Deployment 'checkout' remains at 1/4 available replicas while only 1/4 replicas are updated
Kubernetes service 'kubernetes' currently has no ready API endpoints

causes.message: kube-apiserver, scheduler, and controller-manager depend on shared API, etcd, lease, and control-plane node paths
Multiple control-plane components are failing in the same incident window
Controllers and scheduler cannot reliably renew leases, reach the API, or reconcile workload state
Deployment 'checkout' remains at 1/4 available replicas while only 1/4 replicas are updated

pod:checkout-7d9d4c7f7d-vt6fx: The pod is a downstream workload symptom while control-plane components are unstable
deployment:checkout: Deployment 'checkout' remains at 1/4 available replicas while only 1/4 replicas are updated
pod:kube-apiserver-cp-1: kube-apiserver failed to store Lease object: etcdserver: request timed out
pod:kube-controller-manager-cp-1: kube-controller-manager failed to renew lease kube-system/kube-controller-manager: Put "https://10.96.0.1:443/apis/coordination.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/kube-system/leases/kube-controller-manager": context deadline exceeded
pod:kube-scheduler-cp-1: kube-scheduler leader election lost: failed to update lock kube-system/kube-scheduler: dial tcp 10.96.0.1:443: i/o timeout
service:kubernetes: No ready endpoints back the Kubernetes API Service VIP

likely_causes: etcd latency, quorum loss, or disk pressure is making kube-apiserver and leader-election writes unreliable
Control-plane node network or host-resource pressure is causing API, scheduler, and controller-manager health checks to fail together
The Kubernetes API endpoint or Service VIP is intermittently unreachable from control-plane components
Certificate, manifest, or static-pod configuration drift affected multiple control-plane components during the same incident
suggested_checks: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l tier=control-plane -o wide
kubectl get endpoints kubernetes -n default -o yaml
Check etcd health, control-plane node pressure, and API server /readyz
